response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level pedigree, current hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at Google, and a credible history of high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second). His TypeScript and Node.js depth across multiple roles, combined with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development), align well with the role's engineering culture. However, Python is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from his resume — no mention in skills, projects, or any job description. That gap alone warrants caution at the screening stage. His current Google role began in April 2025, leaving almost no verifiable track record there yet, and the resume provides little evidence of formal stakeholder management or cross-functional coordination at scale.
strengths: Active contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at Google, directly matching the AI-native engineering baseline the role expects
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, building low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching
Consistent Staff-level IC track record with technical strategy responsibilities across multiple companies, paired with strong TypeScript and Node.js expertise
concerns: Python is entirely absent from skills, project descriptions, and all employment history — this is a stated must-have and its omission is a hard blocker without further explanation
Current Google role started April 2025, giving under two months of tenure; claims about AI-agent infrastructure contributions cannot be substantiated from the resume alone
No concrete evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident-response ownership, or on-call operational readiness anywhere in the resume narrative

key_factors.factor: AI-native infrastructure and MCP work at Google
